title: Enable Referential Integrity in a Microsoft SQL Server Database
breadcrumbs: Referential Integrity Management on Microsoft SQL Server
Enable Referential Integrity in a Microsoft SQL Server Database
text: Enable Referential Integrity in a Microsoft SQL Server Database SQL scripts for referential integrity can be generated with the IDE command line switch /genSql . The generated scripts map relationships defined in the application model to declarative constraints and database triggers in the database. To enable referential integrity in the database: Set the connector option DBMS ri in the assignment file. Add the following to the mss_ entity.model _createReferentialIntegrity.sql scripts: where DatabaseName is the name of the database where the entities exist. use DatabaseName go Copy Execute the SQL scripts to create referential integrity. Load the data into the tables. Add the following to the mss_ entity.model _validateReferentialIntegrity.sql scripts: where DatabaseName is the name of the database where the entities exist. use DatabaseName go Copy Execute the SQL scripts to verify the referential integrity. If you need to drop the referential integrity constraints for some reason: Add the following to mss_ entity.model _deleteReferentialIntegrity.sql scripts: use DatabaseName go Copy where DatabaseName is the name of the database where the entities exist. Execute the SQL scripts to drop referential integrity constraints. Related Links Database Scripts For most supported databases, you can generate SQL scripts to create the tables required for a Uniface Repository using the /genSql /createtable command line switch, also known as the Create Table facility.
